response.justification: The candidate has 13+ years of strong polyglot software engineering experience with solid fundamentals in testing, architecture, and distributed systems, but the role specifically requires a C++ developer with deep STL, multi-threading, message broker, and industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443) expertise. The resume shows only a brief 7-month C/C++ stint at National Instruments back in 2014-2015, with the bulk of recent work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of MQTT, OPC UA, industrial protocols, SDL/IEC 62443 practice, or Azure cloud work, which are central to this role.
strengths: Long track record of software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, and architectural thinking
Experience with high-throughput, low-latency, asynchronous, multi-threaded backends (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Polyglot adaptability across embedded, web, mobile, and platform domains
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, over 10 years ago; no recent C++/STL portfolio
No demonstrated exposure to industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), SDL, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850/104, DNP 3.0)
No MQTT, Azure, or message-broker-based large-scale system experience evident in the resume

key_factors.factor: Missing industrial cybersecurity and protocol expertise
Strong general engineering and concurrency background
